commands.name: snap
commands.description: Fetch an RSS or Atom feed and output a clean digest. This is the default command — invoke directly as 'feedsnap <url> [options]'.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: RSS 2.0 or Atom 1.0 feed URL. Required unless --opml is used.

options.name: --opml
options.description: Path to an OPML file containing feed URLs (multi-feed mode). Failed feeds are skipped with a warning; remaining feeds are still returned.

options.name: --limit
options.description: Maximum entries to return. In OPML mode this limit applies per-feed, not across all feeds.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output mode. 'text' emits markdown (human-readable, LLM-friendly); 'json' emits an ACLI-compliant envelope ({ ok, command, version, duration_ms, data }); 'table' emits aligned plain-text columns (TITLE | DATE | URL). Replaces the deprecated --format flag.

options.name: --format
options.description: Deprecated since v0.6.0. Use --output instead. Still accepted: 'markdown' maps to --output text, 'json' maps to legacy JSON output (no ACLI envelope).

options.name: --title
options.description: Include the feed title as an H1 header. Always on in OPML mode (overrides this flag).

options.name: --since
options.description: Only include entries published on or after this date. Accepts YYYY-MM-DD or Nd shorthand (e.g., '2d' = two days ago). Entries with no published date are always included.

options.name: --dedup
options.description: Skip entries already seen in a previous run. Seen entry URLs are tracked in ~/.feedsnap/seen.db (SQLite, created automatically on first use).

options.name: --seen-db
options.description: Custom SQLite database for tracking seen entries (implies --dedup). Useful for per-project seen lists.

examples.intent: Get a markdown digest of the 8 latest entries
Limit to 5 entries and output ACLI JSON envelope
Only show entries from the last 2 days
Only show entries not seen in a previous run
Process multiple feeds from an OPML file (last day only)

commands.name: introspect
commands.description: Output the full command tree as JSON (ACLI v0.1.0 format). Agents should call this for initial capability mapping. Invoke as: feedsnap introspect

examples.intent: Print the full ACLI command tree as JSON

commands.name: skill
commands.description: Output a SKILL.md file for agent bootstrapping (agentskills.io format). Redirect to a file to capture it. Invoke as: feedsnap skill

examples.intent: Write SKILL.md to the current directory
